title: MoonStack — local-first browser tools
heroH1: Tools that stay on your machine.
heroLede: MoonStack builds small, focused tools — Edge extensions and a trading journal — that each do one job well and keep your data on your side of the screen. No accounts, no uploads, no subscriptions.
prin1v: Processing happens in your browser, not on a server.
prin2v: Nothing to sign up for. Install and use.
prin3v: Your files, pages, and screenshots never leave the device.
prin4v: Where there's a paid tier, you buy it once. No subscriptions.
card1desc: Your own local Claude in the Edge sidebar — summarize, translate, compare pages, read images, and search the web, all on your own subscription.
card1note: Uses your Claude. Nothing is routed through us.
card2desc: Capture the visible area, a region, or a full page, then annotate with arrows, boxes, and text and save as PNG — entirely in your browser.
card3desc: Highlight, underline, and annotate PDFs in a local viewer — arrows, boxes, and notes. No upload, no account.
card3note: Your PDF never leaves the tab.
card4desc: Real-time Reddit sentiment for any stock ticker, scored bullish or bearish, with the top posts — right in the side panel.
card4note: Pro adds more posts and an AI summary on your own API key.
card5desc: Decode the latest insider (Form 4) trade into plain language, and redline what management changed between two 10-Q filings — pulled straight from SEC EDGAR, parsed locally.
card5note: Form 4 and a preview are free; the full 10-Q redline is one-time Pro.
workshop:
More small tools
in the workshop.
Suggest one ↗
studioP1: MoonStack is a small independent studio. We make browser extensions that are deliberately narrow: one job, done locally, asking for as few permissions as the job allows.
studioP2: Where a tool has a paid tier, it's a one-time purchase — buy it once, own it. No subscriptions, no telemetry, no ads, no remote code.
studioP3: Questions, bug reports, and feature requests go straight to a human: mumu@moonstack.org
footFine: Each tool's privacy policy is linked on its card above. MoonStack extensions run locally and do not collect personal data; where a tool fetches public data (e.g. SEC filings or Reddit posts), it does so directly from the source at your request. Not investment advice.
